## figma
The official Figma MCP server. Use this server whenever the user wants to create, generate, edit, implement, or sync any design, UI, screen, component, mockup, or visual — in Figma, FigJam, Figma Make, or Figma Slides — and whenever the user mentions Figma or provides a figma.com URL.

This server bridges code and design in both directions, and supports designing from scratch using existing design systems and codebases.

CAPABILITIES:
- Read designs FROM Figma into code (get_design_context, get_screenshot, get_metadata, get_figjam)
- Write designs INTO Figma from code, intent, or existing components (use_figma, generate_figma_design, create_new_file, upload_assets)
- Bridge code and design via Code Connect (get_code_connect_map, add_code_connect_map, list_code_components, get_code_component_info)
- Create diagrams and FigJam content (generate_diagram, get_figjam)

WHEN TO USE THESE TOOLS:
- The user wants to create, mock up, or generate any UI, screen, component, or design — even if Figma isn't named
- The user wants to implement a design as code (design-to-code)
- The user wants to push a page, view, or component into Figma (code-to-design)
- The user wants to update, sync, or edit an existing Figma file
- The user shares a figma.com URL
- The user wants to build or extend a design system, design tokens, or component library
- The user wants to create a diagram in FigJam

SKILLS (load first when available):
- /figma-use — MANDATORY before calling use_figma
- /figma-generate-design — for translating an app page or layout into Figma
- /figma-generate-library — for building a design system in Figma from code
- /figma-code-connect — for mapping Figma components to codebase components
- /figma-use-figjam — for FigJam-specific use_figma flows
- /figma-generate-diagram — MANDATORY before calling generate_diagram

URL PARSING:
Extract fileKey and nodeId from Figma URLs:
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/:fileName?node-id=:nodeId → convert "-" to ":" in nodeId
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/branch/:branchKey/:fileName → use branc… [truncated]

- figma:figma-code-connect: Creates and maintains Figma Code Connect template files that map Figma components to code snippets. Use when the user mentions Code Connect, Figma component mapping, design-to-code translation, or asks to create/update .figma.ts or .figma.js files.
- figma:figma-use-slides: This skill helps agents use Figma's use_figma MCP tool in the Slides context. Can be used alongside figma-use which has foundational context for using the use_figma tool.
- figma:figma-use-figjam: This skill helps agents use Figma's use_figma MCP tool in the FigJam context. Can be used alongside figma-use which has foundational context for using the use_figma tool.
- figma:figma-generate-design: Use this skill alongside figma-use when the task involves translating an application page, view, or multi-section layout into Figma. Triggers: 'write to Figma', 'create in Figma from code', 'push page to Figma', 'take this app/page and build it in Figma', 'create a screen', 'build a landing page in Figma', 'update the Figma screen to match code', 'convert this modal/dialog/drawer/panel to Figma'. This is the preferred workflow skill whenever the user wants to build or update a full page, modal, dialog, drawer, sidebar, panel, or any composed multi-section view in Figma from code or a description. Discovers design system components, variables, and styles from Code Connect files, existing screens, and library search, then imports them and assembles views incrementally section-by-section using design system tokens instead of hardcoded values.
- figma:figma-generate-library: Build or update a professional-grade design system in Figma from a codebase. Use when the user wants to create variables/tokens, build component libraries, create individual components with proper variant sets and variable bindings, set up theming (light/dark modes), document foundations, or reconcile gaps between code and Figma. Also use when the user asks to create or generate any component in Figma — even a single one — since components require proper variable foundations, variant states, and design token bindings to be production-quality. This skill teaches WHAT to build and in WHAT ORDER — it complements the `figma-use` skill which teaches HOW to call the Plugin API. Both skills should be loaded together.
- figma:figma-generate-diagram: MANDATORY prerequisite — load this skill BEFORE every `generate_diagram` tool call. NEVER call `generate_diagram` directly without loading this skill first. Trigger whenever the user asks to create, generate, draw, render, sketch, or build a diagram — flowchart, architecture diagram, sequence diagram, ERD or entity-relationship diagram, state diagram or state machine, gantt chart, or timeline. Also trigger when the user mentions Mermaid syntax or wants a system architecture, decision tree, dependency graph, API call flow, auth handshake, schema, or pipeline visualized in FigJam. Routes to type-specific guidance, sets universal Mermaid constraints, and tells you when to use a different diagram type or skip the tool entirely (mindmaps, pie charts, class diagrams, etc.).
- figma:figma-create-new-file: **MANDATORY prerequisite** — you MUST invoke this skill BEFORE every `create_new_file` tool call. NEVER call `create_new_file` directly without loading this skill first. Trigger whenever the user wants a new blank Figma file — a new design, FigJam, or Slides file — or when you need a fresh file before calling `use_figma`. Usage — /figma-create-new-file [editorType] [fileName] (e.g. /figma-create-new-file figjam My Whiteboard, /figma-create-new-file slides Q3 Review)
- figma:figma-use: **MANDATORY prerequisite** — you MUST invoke this skill BEFORE every `use_figma` tool call. NEVER call `use_figma` directly without loading this skill first. Skipping it causes common, hard-to-debug failures. Trigger whenever the user wants to perform a write action or a unique read action that requires JavaScript execution in the Figma file context — e.g. create/edit/delete nodes, set up variables or tokens, build components and variants, modify auto-layout or fills, bind variables to properties, or inspect file structure programmatically.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# Architecture — workspace repo map
2	
3	This file is a **map of the repos in this workspace** — what each one does, what it depends on, and how they talk to each other. Not every repo below will be present locally at any given time; only the ones currently pulled appear on disk. Add to this file as new repos are pulled; remove nothing (keeping retired entries is fine as long as they're labeled).
4	
5	**Before working in any repo that ends in `*-core-lib`, read the "core-lib framework conventions" section at the bottom** — the folder layout, decorators, and data-layer split are identical across all of them.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## Repos currently tracked
10	
11	| Repo | Role | Language | Depends on |
12	|---|---|---|---|
13	| `ob-love-admin-client` | Admin web UI | React (JS) | `ob-love-ui` (shared components); talks to `ob-love-admin-backend` over HTTP |
14	| `ob-love-ui` | Shared React component library (`@ob-love/ui`) | React (JS) + SCSS | MUI, dayjs, react-markdown, react-phone-input-2 (peer deps) |
15	| `ob-love-admin-backend` | Admin HTTP API; hosts `admin_core_lib` | Python (Flask + SQLAlchemy) | `form-core-lib`, `pay-core-lib`, `promise-core-lib`, `custom_field_core_lib`, `objective_love_core_lib`, `core-lib` |
16	| `form-core-lib` | Generic form / page / field persistence | Python (SQLAlchemy) | `core-lib` |
17	| `promise-core-lib` | Generic promise / accountability infrastructure (4-role model, transactional outbox, reminder/at-risk jobs, Temporal worker) — **single owner of the `promise` table** | Python (SQLAlchemy) | `core-lib` |
18	| `funnel-core-lib` | Funnel / stage / lead persistence + scoring + history | Python (SQLAlchemy) | `core-lib` |
19	| `pay-core-lib` | Payment gateway integration (PayPlus / Yaadpay) + transaction persistence | Python (SQLAlchemy) | `core-lib` |
20	| `interest_core_lib` | User-interest catalog (TMDB / Spotify / Google Books / Wikiart / internal Solr) + matchmaking enrichment | Python (Solr + HTTP clients) | `core-lib` |
21	| `core-lib` | The framework every `*-core-lib` extends | Python | — (foundation) |
22	
23	Dependency arrows (consumer → dependency):
24	
25	```
26	ob-love-admin-client ──HTTP──▶ ob-love-admin-backend
27	       │                              │
28	       └──▶ ob-love-ui                ├──▶ form-core-lib ──▶ core-lib
29	                                      ├──▶ funnel-core-lib ──▶ core-lib
30	                                      ├──▶ pay-core-lib ──▶ core-lib
31	                                      ├──▶ promise-core-lib ──▶ core-lib
32	                                      ├──▶ custom_field_core_lib ──▶ core-lib
33	                                      ├──▶ objective_love_core_lib ──▶ core-lib
34	                                      └──▶ core-lib
35	
36	Out-of-band data flow (CDC pipeline, see "Data Pipeline" section):
37	
38	postgres ──Debezium──▶ Kafka topics ──KStreams join──▶ joined-user topic ──ES sink──▶ Elasticsearch
39	                                                                                              ▲
40	                                                          ob-love-admin-backend reads ────────┘
41	```
42	
43	Packages marked as installed-only (not in this workspace yet) — `custom_field_core_lib`, `objective_love_core_lib` — ship via pip and are consumed but not edited here.
44	
45	---
46	
47	## `ob-love-admin-client` — React admin UI
48	
49	**What it does.** The admin dashboard. Form builder, user management, custom-field editor, submission viewer.
50	
51	**Stack.** React, Zustand for state, MUI for widgets, `react-beautiful-dnd` for drag-and-drop, `i18n` for locales (en / he / nl).
52	
53	**Top-level layout** (relative to `src/`):
54	- `pages/` — route components. One folder per top-level route.
55	- `components/` — shared UI. Leaf folders often pair a `.js` and a `.scss`.
56	- `store/` — Zustand stores. `useXxxStore` hook; `useXxxStore.getState()` outside React.
57	- `helpers.js`, `constants.js`, `i18n.js` — cross-cutting utilities.
58	- `assets/locale/{en,he,nl}/i18n.js` — translation bundles.
59	
60	**Talks to backend via** `src/network/` (REST wrappers). Responses are shaped by `admin_core_lib`'s services (post-`ResultToDict`) so the client sees plain dicts.
61	
62	**Known conventions.**
63	- **Enums and constant maps live on the backend, not the client.** Anything that has a Python `enum` on the backend (license plan, billing cycle, task status, custom-field type, …) ships to the client via `AdminSystemService.get_admin()` → `update_constants(constants, <EnumClass>)` and is read off `useSystemStore.getState().constants.<snake_case_enum_name>`. The placeholder dicts in [`systemStore.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/store/system/systemStore.js)'s initial state (`license_plan: {}`, `task_status: {}`, `billing_cycle: {}`, …) are the contract — one entry per registered enum, hydrated on app init. **Do not redeclare the same key/value pairs in a frontend module** (e.g. `BILLING_CYCLE_KEY = { MONTHLY: 'monthly', YEARLY: 'yearly' }` next to a backend `BillingCycle` enum) — when the backend enum changes the client silently drifts. If the client needs a specific value at compile time for routing/styling, read it from `systemStore.constants.<enum>` at the call site or accept the raw string from the API response.
64	- **Org-level feature flags live on `organizationStore.userOrganizationDetails`** (`is_mobile_app_available`, `is_lgbtq`, `is_use_age_range`, `collect_first_name`, `collect_last_name`, …). They gate UI visibility across many pages — fields, filters, panels, the workflow trigger config — and are **propagated as props from the page that subscribes via `useOrganizationStore` down to leaf components**, not re-read from the store inside each leaf. Pattern: page reads `organizationStore.userOrganizationDetails.<flag>` once, passes `<flag>={...}` down; leaves declare a matching default-prop (`is_mobile_app_available: false`) and use the AGENTS-prescribed extract-outside-JSX form — `const xEl = props.<flag> && renderX();` — to conditionally render. Canonical leaves: [`UserInteractionTab.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/userFilters/userSearchFiltersTabs/UserInteractionTab/UserInteractionTab.js), [`AdvancedInformationTab.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/userFilters/userSearchFiltersTabs/AdvancedInformationTab/AdvancedInformationTab.js), [`WorkflowWhenUserRegistered.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/workflow/when/WorkflowWhenUserRegistered/WorkflowWhenUserRegistered.js). For *field-pair swaps* (not just visibility) the flag also drives helpers like `getActiveAgeFieldPair` — see "Flag-gated user-field pairs" below.
65	- Zustand outside a component: always `useStore.getState()`. The hook form only works in render.
66	- Controlled inputs: never pass `value={null}` / `value={undefined}` — React flips to uncontrolled and the DOM value gets stale.
67	- **[`MarkdownEditor`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/MarkdownEditor/MarkdownEditor.js) (wraps `@mdxeditor/editor`) — three invariants for the wrapper, all required.** (1) `value` is INITIAL markdown, not a controlled value. The wrapper caches `props.value` into a `useRef` on mount and feeds that ref to MDXEditor's `markdown` prop. MDXEditor (Lexical under the hood) treats `markdown` as reactive: changing it re-parses the string and rebuilds the entire `EditorState`. Looping the editor's own `onChange` output back into `markdown` causes a full reset on every keystroke — caret snaps to position 0 (typed characters appear stuck on the first line) and pressing Enter inside a list aborts before the new `<li>` commits (bullets break). (2) The `plugins` array reference must be stable across renders. MDXEditor rebuilds its Lexical realm whenever the `plugins` prop's identity changes, which is just as destructive as a `markdown` reset — caret detaches from text, characters land in wrong order, in-progress lists/headings collapse. The wrapper memoizes the array with `useMemo([], [])` and hoists `renderToolbar` (the `toolbarContents` callback for `toolbarPlugin`) to module scope so its identity is constant. Do not move `headingsPlugin()`/`listsPlugin()`/etc. back inline into the JSX `plugins={[…]}` literal, and do not define the toolbar callback inside the component body — both reintroduce the bug. `<DiffSourceToggleWrapper>` must wrap the other toolbar items as children (it's how source/diff mode hides the rich-mode buttons); a self-closing `<DiffSourceToggleWrapper/>` is a misuse. (3) **`[App.scss](ob-love-admin-client/src/App.scss)` applies `label, span { display: inline-block }` globally — Lexical wraps every text node in a `<span>`, so this rule alone is enough to make the editor look broken** (typed text won't reflow, the caret renders detached from the inserted characters, Enter/bullets/lists fail to lay out). [`MarkdownEditor.scss`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/MarkdownEditor/MarkdownEditor.scss) carries a scoped reset — `.md-editor-wrap [contenteditable="true"] span { display: inline }` — that restores natural inline flow inside the editable area only (the toolbar's spans keep their `inline-block` layout). Do not remove that override, and do not loosen the `[contenteditable="true"]` qualifier — broadening it to all `.md-editor-wrap span` would break the toolbar. If the global rule is ever removed from `App.scss`, the override can come out too. Consumers pass `onChange` to capture edits and `onBlur` to persist — the canonical pattern is in [`UserPanelStoryTab.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/userPanel/UserPanelStoryTab/UserPanelStoryTab.js) and [`UserPanelMatchExplanation.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/userPanel/UserPanelMatchExplanation/UserPanelMatchExplanation.js). To reload the editor with a different initial value (e.g. switching to a different user's story), remount via a React `key` prop on `MarkdownEditor`; do not attempt to swap the value by changing `props.value`.
68	- User-facing field labels resolve via `getFieldName(isUserField, fieldData)` in `components/CustomField/helpers.js` → `users.fields.${label}` for user fields, raw `label` for custom fields.
69	- Zustand `persist` middleware (e.g. `useLocalStorageStore`) hydrates from `localStorage` **synchronously** at store creation. For UI state that drives transitioned/animated components (MUI Accordion, Collapse, Modal, Dialog), read the persisted value via a **lazy `useState` initializer** — `useState(() => useXxxStore.getState().getFoo(key))` — so the component's first render already has the saved value. Reading it inside a `useEffect` causes a `default → saved` state transition on mount, which triggers the component's open/close animation on first load.

118	- **`SEARCH_FIELDS` are NOT searchable fields — easy to confuse.** `SEARCH_FIELDS` in [`fieldOrderHelpers.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/User/fieldOrderHelpers.js) (`age_from`/`age_to`, `age_years_younger`/`age_years_older`, `height_from_cm`/`height_to_cm`, `want_smoking`, `location`, `location_radius`) is the list of user **preference** fields — what the user is *looking for in matches*, hard-wired into `buildFindMatchesFilter` on every find-matches call. They never live in `searchableFields`, never get the icon, and never appear in the searchable menu. The constant is module-private to `fieldOrderHelpers.js` — don't export it.
119	- **Find-matches flow.** [`Users.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/pages/Users/Users.js)'s `findMatches(userId, userData)`: (1) build base filter via `buildFindMatchesFilter(userData, {is_lgbtq, is_use_age_range, default_orientation, systemConstants})` — pulls the user's preference fields (`SEARCH_FIELDS`) and gender/orientation logic. (2) Read `searchableFields` from `useUiSettingsStore`. (3) `applySearchableOverrides(baseFilter, searchableFields)` merges in the per-field overrides as `merged['custom_field_<id>'] = { value, type }`. (4) Pass to `usersStore.onFiltersSelected(filterData, false, true, userId)` with `isFindMatches=true`.
120	- **DROPDOWN / MULTI_SELECT array contract — must-know.** `filtersSelected['custom_field_<id>'].value` for DROPDOWN/MULTI_SELECT must be an **array** — the chip renderer at [`UserSearchQuery.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/userFilters/UserSearchQuery/UserSearchQuery.js) line ~514 does `value.map(...).join(', ')`, and the existing filter UI at [`CustomFieldsTab.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/userFilters/userSearchFiltersTabs/CustomFieldsTab/CustomFieldsTab.js) writes them with `multiple={true}`. The `MarkSearchableDialog` UI stores DROPDOWN as a single string (`LabeledDropdown multiple={false}`) and MULTI_SELECT as an array, so `applySearchableOverrides` normalizes by wrapping non-array values to `[value]` for both types before merging. Without that wrap → `TypeError: value.map is not a function`.
121	- **UI surface.** [`UserDetailsPanel.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/User/UserDetailsPanel/UserDetailsPanel.js) renders the magnifying-glass `SearchableIcon` ([`assets/icons/SearchableIcon.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/assets/icons/SearchableIcon.js)) inside `.user-details-menu-wrap`, to the **left** of the three-dot menu (not inside the field-name area), when `isFieldSearchable(field, searchableFields)` is true. The row's menu is composed as `[...categorySubCategoryMenuItems, ...buildSearchableFieldMenuItems(field, searchableFields)]`, so existing menu items stay first and "mark / edit / remove searchable" append.
122	- **Comparison options — currently equals only.** `comparisonOptions` in [`MarkSearchableDialog.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/User/MarkSearchableDialog/MarkSearchableDialog.js) returns `[_opt('equals')]` for all four eligible types. The `COMPARISON_LABELS` map and the `general.contains` / `general.startsWith` / `general.endsWith` / `general.in` i18n keys are intentionally kept — re-introducing other comparisons is just adding entries to that array. **Backend currently only honors equality on custom fields**, so even if the UI sent another comparison it would be ignored at match time.
123	- **Pruning stale entries.** [`fieldsStore.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/store/fields/fieldsStore.js) calls `useUiSettingsStore.getState().pruneSearchableForCustomFields(customFieldsData)` after init / delete / reorder. The store action delegates to [`pruneSearchableForCustomFields`](ob-love-admin-client/src/store/uiSettings/helpers.js) which drops any entry whose `custom_field_<id>` no longer exists or whose stored `type` no longer matches the current field's type. The helper returns `{ cleaned, changed }` — the action short-circuits the store update when `changed === false` to avoid spurious rerenders.
124	- **Dialog wiring.** `showMarkSearchableDialog(field, existing, onSubmit)` in [`store/window/helpers.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/store/window/helpers.js) wraps the dialog in `DialogWithActions`. Save is gated by `validate(params) → !isEmptyValue(params.value)`. The validate fallback to `initial?.value` when `params.value === undefined` exists so the Save button doesn't flicker disabled→enabled when *re*opening a previously-marked field — `MarkSearchableDialog`'s mount effect seeds `dialogParams` asynchronously, so the first validate pass before the effect runs would otherwise fail. The dialog's preview-pill chips use `.search-query-wrapper` / `.query-phrase` from the global `SearchQuery.scss`, with a scoped override in [`MarkSearchableDialog.scss`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/User/MarkSearchableDialog/MarkSearchableDialog.scss) to allow the chips to wrap below a long field label (the global `.query-phrase` is fixed-height inline-flex).
125	
126	**Form-builder helpers** (all in [`components/Form/helpers.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/Form/helpers.js)) — every form-builder component goes through these, do not reimplement:
127	
128	| Helper | Use when |
129	|---|---|
130	| `buildNewFormPageField(rawField, systemConstants)` | A raw `CustomField`/`UserFieldDefinition` from the palette is being turned into a `form_page_field` row (drag-drop, menu-add, mandatory-field seeding). Returns the full shape: `target_field_id`, `target_field_type`, `name`, `meta_data`, `is_user_field`, and the aliased `custom_field_*` / `user_field_*` columns so pre-save rendering matches post-reload. **Single source of truth — all four "add field" code paths call it.** |
131	| `isFormPageFieldUserField(field, systemConstants)` | Deciding user vs custom for a `form_page_field` row. Handles both saved rows (uses `target_field_type`) and unsaved rows (uses `is_user_field`). |
132	| `getFormPageFieldLabel(field, isUserField)` | Producing the i18n key for a `form_page_field`'s display label. Walks `user_field_label` → `custom_field_label` → `label` → `field.name`. |
133	| `canFieldBeHidden(field, allowed_types)` | Checking whether a field can be dropped onto a hidden page. Reads `.type` (raw palette field) OR `.custom_field_type` / `.user_field_type` (form_page_field row). |
134	| `isMandatoryFieldsPresent(pageData, mandatoryFields)` | Warning-message gate in `formEditStore.updateWarningMessage`. Checks `field.user_field_label || field.label` against `mandatoryUserFields`. |
135	| `buildUserFields(fieldsData, is_lgbtq)`, `buildMandatoryUserFields(fieldsData, is_lgbtq)` | Populating the palette and mandatory-field list at init. Filters by `field.label` (not `field.meta_data.key`). |
136	
137	**FormPage / FormPageFieldDisplay / renderFormField** — layered responsibility:
138	- `FormPage.js` (per-page renderer) computes `isUserField` once via the helper and passes it down as a prop.
139	- `FormPageFieldDisplay.js` is **dumb** — takes `isUserField` from props, calls `getFormPageFieldLabel` for its label, and uses that label for both the visible label and the disabled placeholder input.
140	- `renderFormField.js` (the end-user form renderer) computes `isUserField` + `fieldLabel` once at the top and uses `fieldLabel` for every `label` / `placeholder` across all field-type branches (text, date, dropdown, photo, range sub-fields, orientation).
141	
142	**Rename note (important for next session).** The `form_page_field.field_type` column was renamed to **`target_field_type`** across the stack, and the enum class `FormFieldType` was renamed to **`FormTargetFieldType`**. Consequences:
143	- Frontend reads `field.target_field_type` (saved) or `field.is_user_field` (unsaved).
144	- systemConstants key is `form_target_field_type` (derived from the snake-cased class name).
145	- `systemConstants.field_type` on the client is a **different** enum — `CustomField.FieldType` (TEXT / NUMBER / DATE / DROPDOWN / YES_NO / PHOTO / …). Don't conflate the two.
146	
147	**Drag & drop flow** (form editor): four entry points end up calling `buildNewFormPageField`:
148	- `FormEdit.onDragEnd` → regular page: inline `newField = buildNewFormPageField(...)`, then `formEditStore.updateFieldsOrder(..., newField)`.
149	- `FormEdit.onDragEnd` → hidden page: `formEditStore.updateFieldsWithHiddenPage(...)` builds it internally.
150	- Menu "Add to page": `formEditStore.addFieldToPage(...)` builds it.
151	- Menu "Add to hidden page": `formEditStore.addFieldToHiddenPage(...)` builds it.
152	
153	---
154	
155	## `ob-love-ui` — shared React component library
156	
157	**What it does.** The `@ob-love/ui` package — reusable UI primitives, helpers, utilities, and SCSS tokens consumed by `ob-love-admin-client` (and future ob-love apps). Anything that is genuinely cross-app and domain-free moves here; we'll keep adding to it as we go.
158	
159	**Stack.** React 18, MUI 5, dayjs, react-markdown, `react-phone-input-2`, `@uiw/react-md-editor`. Built with `tsup`; published locally via `yalc` (`npm run publish:local` → `npm run dev` watches and pushes).
160	
161	**Top-level layout** (relative to `ob-love-ui/`):
162	- `src/components/<Name>/<Name>.{js,scss}` — one folder per component (see `src/index.js` for the export list: `Tag`, `Badge`, `Heading`, `Loading`, `AvatarInfoSmall`, `IconLabel`, `AutocompleteDropdown`, `DropDownMenu`, `EditMode*` editable inputs, `Labeled*` inputs, `LabeledAddressField`, `DateRangePicker`, …).
163	- `src/assets/svg/` — illustration SVGs the lib ships.
164	- `src/utils.js`, `src/i18n.js`, `src/QueueCallbackRunner.js`, `src/theme.js` — common helpers, the i18n setter (`setI18n`), an async queue runner, and the MUI theme factory (`createObLoveTheme`, `inputOptionsNumberSpinner`, `tabsUnderLine`).
165	- **i18n bridge contract.** `src/i18n.js` is *not* a re-export of i18next — it's a small proxy whose default export only exposes the methods explicitly forwarded to the client-injected instance (today: `t`, `dir`). The client wires its singleton in via `setI18n(i18n)` at startup (`ob-love-admin-client/src/i18n.js`), and library code reads through the proxy. If a library file needs another i18next method (`language`, `changeLanguage`, `exists`, …), **add it to the bridge first** — calling it on the default export without proxying it throws `i18n.<x> is not a function` at runtime. Never `import i18next from 'i18next'` directly from inside `ob-love-ui` — the symlink/yalc dedupe trap is the whole reason the bridge exists.
166	- `styles/constants.scss` — design tokens (`$COLOR-*`, `$SIZE-*`, `$FONT-*`, plus the JS `:export` block consumed via `import constants from '@ob-love/ui/styles/constants.scss'`). All hex/px constants for the whole stack live here — see the SCSS Styling rules in [ob-love-admin-client/AGENTS.md](ob-love-admin-client/AGENTS.md) for the do-not-hardcode policy.
167	- `styles/common.scss`, `styles/img-field.scss`, `styles/scrollbar.scss` — shared global styles.
168	
169	**Consumer pattern in `ob-love-admin-client`.**
170	- Imports are `import { Loading, AutocompleteDropdown, LabeledTextField, ... } from '@ob-love/ui'`. Constants come in via `import constants from '@ob-love/ui/styles/constants.scss'`.
171	- Don't duplicate a primitive locally if a `@ob-love/ui` export already covers it — extend the upstream component or wrap it instead. Domain-named props don't belong on these primitives (see "Generic UI components must not know domain concepts" in the AGENTS file).
172	- When promoting a primitive from `ob-love-admin-client` to `ob-love-ui`: move the component folder, add an export to `ob-love-ui/src/index.js`, run `npm run publish:local`, and update the client imports. Domain helpers (anything that references admin/users/forms/etc.) stay in the client.
173	
174	**Component API gotchas.**
175	- **`LabeledToggleButton` takes `initialValue`, not `value`.** Despite the name, it behaves like a controlled input — internal state is re-synced whenever the `initialValue` prop changes (`useEffect` in [LabeledToggleButton.js](ob-love-ui/src/components/LabeledToggleButton/LabeledToggleButton.js)). Passing `value={...}` silently does nothing: the prop is ignored, `internalValue` stays `undefined`, `ToggleButtonGroup` receives no `value`, and the active button never highlights even though `onChange` keeps firing. Every caller in `ob-love-admin-client` uses `initialValue=` — match that.
176	- **`AutocompleteDropdown` keeps the popup open after selection in `multiple` mode.** [AutocompleteDropdown.js](ob-love-ui/src/components/AutocompleteDropdown/AutocompleteDropdown.js) hard-codes `disableCloseOnSelect={props.multiple}` on the underlying MUI `<Autocomplete>`. MUI's own default for that flag is `false` — meaning the popup closes on every option click, even in `multiple` mode, which fires `onClose` and (for paginated wrappers like [UserSelectAutocompleteDropdown](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/UserSelectAutocompleteDropdown/UserSelectAutocompleteDropdown.js)) tears down the in-flight `searchResults` mid-selection. The wrapper's `onClose` handler runs in the same React batch as the parent's `setFormValues` from `onChange`; if it clears the option list before the chip renders, the just-selected id resolves to nothing and no chip mounts. Rules for any multi-select wrapper that holds local state tied to the open dropdown (search results, pagination, accumulated pages):
177	  1. **Rely on `disableCloseOnSelect` staying on for `multiple`** — don't override it back to `false` per call site.
178	  2. **Don't put aggressive teardown in the wrapper's `onClose`.** No `setSearchResults([])`, no `setInputValue('')`. Only clear cross-component side effects (e.g. store filters) there.
179	  3. **Don't write to `inputValue` on `onInputChange` `reason !== 'input'`.** With `disableCloseOnSelect` on, MUI fires `'reset'` not just after a selection but also on options/value re-syncs that happen mid-typing — clearing `inputValue` there wipes the user's text on every keystroke. Gate `setInputValue(value)` behind `reason === 'input'`; leave the rest alone.
180	
181	**Peer-dep contract.** Everything MUI/dayjs/react-markdown/etc. is a `peerDependency` — `ob-love-ui` does not bundle React or MUI; the consuming app supplies them. Adding a new heavy dep to this lib is a deliberate decision (it grows the peer-dep surface of every consumer), so prefer pulling in functionality the consumer already has.
182	
183	---
184	
185	## `pay-core-lib` — payment gateway integration
186	
187	**What it does.** Captures and persists payment transactions from the PayPlus gateway (legacy Yaadpay tables also tracked). Provides the URL generator for hosted checkout, the success/verification webhook handler, and the read queries the admin app needs for billing.
188	
189	**Owned tables.** `payment_pay_plus_info`, `payment_info_hyp` (legacy Yaadpay; not actively written to).
190	
191	**Key modules.**
192	- [`pay_core_lib/pay_core_lib.py`](pay-core-lib/pay_core_lib/pay_core_lib.py) — composition root. Exposes `self.payment` (a `PaymentPayPlusService`) and `self.auth` (the `ClientPayPlus` HTTP client). Parent apps reach payment via `<their_core_lib>.pay.payment.<method>(...)`.
193	- [`pay_core_lib/data_layers/service/payment_pay_plus_service.py`](pay-core-lib/pay_core_lib/data_layers/service/payment_pay_plus_service.py) — `get_url(...)` (hosted-checkout URL), `payment_success(...)` (webhook handler — verifies, persists, fires `EVENT_PAYMENT_SUCCESS`), `all_recurring_payments()`, `all_by_project(project_id)`, `get_latest_by_project(project_id)` (the billing-page reads), `update(id, data)`.
194	- [`pay_core_lib/data_layers/data_access/payment_pay_plus_data_access.py`](pay-core-lib/pay_core_lib/data_layers/data_access/payment_pay_plus_data_access.py) — soft-delete CRUD plus the `all_by_project` / `get_latest_by_project` / `all_recurring_payments` queries.
195	- [`pay_core_lib/data_layers/client/pay_plus/client_pay_plus.py`](pay-core-lib/pay_core_lib/data_layers/client/pay_plus/client_pay_plus.py) — outbound HTTP to PayPlus.
196	- `pay_core_lib/observer/pay_listener.py` + `pay_observer_listener.py` — observer pattern; consumers register a `PayListener` via `PayCoreLib.attach_pay_listener(...)` to react to `EVENT_PAYMENT_SUCCESS`.
197	
198	**`pay_core_lib.constants.CurrencyCode` + `CurrencySymbol`.** Two paired enums in [`pay_core_lib/constants.py`](pay-core-lib/pay_core_lib/constants.py). `CurrencyCode` is the storage/transport enum (`ILS = 'ILS'`, `USD = 'USD'`, `EUR = 'EUR'`, `GBP = 'GBP'`) — `.value` is what gets written to the `currency` VARCHAR column and sent to the PayPlus gateway. `CurrencySymbol` is a plain class-based enum whose member names mirror `CurrencyCode` — a module-level `assert set(CurrencySymbol.__members__) == set(CurrencyCode.__members__)` catches drift at import time. Pair them via `CurrencySymbol[code.name].value` → `'$'` / `'₪'` / `'€'` / `'£'`. Both ship to the client via `AdminSystemService.get_admin()` → `update_constants(...)` so the client sees them as `systemStore.constants.currency_code` and `systemStore.constants.currency_symbol`. **Not the same map as `objective_love_core_lib.constants.package_constants.currency_symbols`** (plural, integer-keyed, used by package pricing) — pay-side flows always read from these two enums.
199	
200	`CurrencyCode` used to be nested inside `PaymentPayPlusInfo` for historical reasons; it was lifted to `constants.py` so `CurrencySymbol` can reference it without a circular import (the entity already imports `DEFAULT_SYSTEM` from `constants.py`). All consumers — `payment_pay_plus_service`, `client_pay_plus`, `admin_payment_service`, `admin_billing_service` — now import `CurrencyCode` directly from `pay_core_lib.constants`.
