# Architecture — workspace repo map

This file is a **map of the repos in this workspace** — what each one does, what it depends on, and how they talk to each other. Not every repo below will be present locally at any given time; only the ones currently pulled appear on disk. Add to this file as new repos are pulled; remove nothing (keeping retired entries is fine as long as they're labeled).

**Before working in any repo that ends in `*-core-lib`, read the "core-lib framework conventions" section at the bottom** — the folder layout, decorators, and data-layer split are identical across all of them.

---

## Repos currently tracked

| Repo | Role | Language | Depends on |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ob-love-admin-client` | Admin web UI | React (JS) | `ob-love-ui` (shared components); talks to `ob-love-admin-backend` over HTTP |
| `ob-love-ui` | Shared React component library (`@ob-love/ui`) | React (JS) + SCSS | MUI, dayjs, react-markdown, react-phone-input-2 (peer deps) |
| `ob-love-admin-backend` | Admin HTTP API; hosts `admin_core_lib` | Python (Flask + SQLAlchemy) | `form-core-lib`, `pay-core-lib`, `promise-core-lib`, `custom_field_core_lib`, `objective_love_core_lib`, `core-lib` |
| `form-core-lib` | Generic form / page / field persistence | Python (SQLAlchemy) | `core-lib` |
| `promise-core-lib` | Generic promise / accountability infrastructure (4-role model, transactional outbox, reminder/at-risk jobs, Temporal worker) — **single owner of the `promise` table** | Python (SQLAlchemy) | `core-lib` |
| `funnel-core-lib` | Funnel / stage / lead persistence + scoring + history | Python (SQLAlchemy) | `core-lib` |
| `pay-core-lib` | Payment gateway integration (PayPlus / Yaadpay) + transaction persistence | Python (SQLAlchemy) | `core-lib` |
| `interest_core_lib` | User-interest catalog (TMDB / Spotify / Google Books / Wikiart / internal Solr) + matchmaking enrichment | Python (Solr + HTTP clients) | `core-lib` |
| `core-lib` | The framework every `*-core-lib` extends | Python | — (foundation) |

Dependency arrows (consumer → dependency):

```
ob-love-admin-client ──HTTP──▶ ob-love-admin-backend
       │                              │
       └──▶ ob-love-ui                ├──▶ form-core-lib ──▶ core-lib
                                      ├──▶ funnel-core-lib ──▶ core-lib
                                      ├──▶ pay-core-lib ──▶ core-lib
                                      ├──▶ promise-core-lib ──▶ core-lib
                                      ├──▶ custom_field_core_lib ──▶ core-lib
                                      ├──▶ objective_love_core_lib ──▶ core-lib
                                      └──▶ core-lib

Out-of-band data flow (CDC pipeline, see "Data Pipeline" section):

postgres ──Debezium──▶ Kafka topics ──KStreams join──▶ joined-user topic ──ES sink──▶ Elasticsearch
                                                                                              ▲
                                                          ob-love-admin-backend reads ────────┘
```

Packages marked as installed-only (not in this workspace yet) — `custom_field_core_lib`, `objective_love_core_lib` — ship via pip and are consumed but not edited here.

---

## `ob-love-admin-client` — React admin UI

**What it does.** The admin dashboard. Form builder, user management, custom-field editor, submission viewer.

**Stack.** React, Zustand for state, MUI for widgets, `react-beautiful-dnd` for drag-and-drop, `i18n` for locales (en / he / nl).

**Top-level layout** (relative to `src/`):
- `pages/` — route components. One folder per top-level route.
- `components/` — shared UI. Leaf folders often pair a `.js` and a `.scss`.
- `store/` — Zustand stores. `useXxxStore` hook; `useXxxStore.getState()` outside React.
- `helpers.js`, `constants.js`, `i18n.js` — cross-cutting utilities.
- `assets/locale/{en,he,nl}/i18n.js` — translation bundles.

**Talks to backend via** `src/network/` (REST wrappers). Responses are shaped by `admin_core_lib`'s services (post-`ResultToDict`) so the client sees plain dicts.

**Known conventions.**
- **Enums and constant maps live on the backend, not the client.** Anything that has a Python `enum` on the backend (license plan, billing cycle, task status, custom-field type, …) ships to the client via `AdminSystemService.get_admin()` → `update_constants(constants, <EnumClass>)` and is read off `useSystemStore.getState().constants.<snake_case_enum_name>`. The placeholder dicts in [`systemStore.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/store/system/systemStore.js)'s initial state (`license_plan: {}`, `task_status: {}`, `billing_cycle: {}`, …) are the contract — one entry per registered enum, hydrated on app init. **Do not redeclare the same key/value pairs in a frontend module** (e.g. `BILLING_CYCLE_KEY = { MONTHLY: 'monthly', YEARLY: 'yearly' }` next to a backend `BillingCycle` enum) — when the backend enum changes the client silently drifts. If the client needs a specific value at compile time for routing/styling, read it from `systemStore.constants.<enum>` at the call site or accept the raw string from the API response.
- **Org-level feature flags live on `organizationStore.userOrganizationDetails`** (`is_mobile_app_available`, `is_lgbtq`, `is_use_age_range`, `collect_first_name`, `collect_last_name`, …). They gate UI visibility across many pages — fields, filters, panels, the workflow trigger config — and are **propagated as props from the page that subscribes via `useOrganizationStore` down to leaf components**, not re-read from the store inside each leaf. Pattern: page reads `organizationStore.userOrganizationDetails.<flag>` once, passes `<flag>={...}` down; leaves declare a matching default-prop (`is_mobile_app_available: false`) and use the AGENTS-prescribed extract-outside-JSX form — `const xEl = props.<flag> && renderX();` — to conditionally render. Canonical leaves: [`UserInteractionTab.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/userFilters/userSearchFiltersTabs/UserInteractionTab/UserInteractionTab.js), [`AdvancedInformationTab.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/userFilters/userSearchFiltersTabs/AdvancedInformationTab/AdvancedInformationTab.js), [`WorkflowWhenUserRegistered.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/workflow/when/WorkflowWhenUserRegistered/WorkflowWhenUserRegistered.js). For *field-pair swaps* (not just visibility) the flag also drives helpers like `getActiveAgeFieldPair` — see "Flag-gated user-field pairs" below.
- Zustand outside a component: always `useStore.getState()`. The hook form only works in render.
- Controlled inputs: never pass `value={null}` / `value={undefined}` — React flips to uncontrolled and the DOM value gets stale.
- **[`MarkdownEditor`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/MarkdownEditor/MarkdownEditor.js) (wraps `@mdxeditor/editor`) — three invariants for the wrapper, all required.** (1) `value` is INITIAL markdown, not a controlled value. The wrapper caches `props.value` into a `useRef` on mount and feeds that ref to MDXEditor's `markdown` prop. MDXEditor (Lexical under the hood) treats `markdown` as reactive: changing it re-parses the string and rebuilds the entire `EditorState`. Looping the editor's own `onChange` output back into `markdown` causes a full reset on every keystroke — caret snaps to position 0 (typed characters appear stuck on the first line) and pressing Enter inside a list aborts before the new `<li>` commits (bullets break). (2) The `plugins` array reference must be stable across renders. MDXEditor rebuilds its Lexical realm whenever the `plugins` prop's identity changes, which is just as destructive as a `markdown` reset — caret detaches from text, characters land in wrong order, in-progress lists/headings collapse. The wrapper memoizes the array with `useMemo([], [])` and hoists `renderToolbar` (the `toolbarContents` callback for `toolbarPlugin`) to module scope so its identity is constant. Do not move `headingsPlugin()`/`listsPlugin()`/etc. back inline into the JSX `plugins={[…]}` literal, and do not define the toolbar callback inside the component body — both reintroduce the bug. `<DiffSourceToggleWrapper>` must wrap the other toolbar items as children (it's how source/diff mode hides the rich-mode buttons); a self-closing `<DiffSourceToggleWrapper/>` is a misuse. (3) **`[App.scss](ob-love-admin-client/src/App.scss)` applies `label, span { display: inline-block }` globally — Lexical wraps every text node in a `<span>`, so this rule alone is enough to make the editor look broken** (typed text won't reflow, the caret renders detached from the inserted characters, Enter/bullets/lists fail to lay out). [`MarkdownEditor.scss`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/MarkdownEditor/MarkdownEditor.scss) carries a scoped reset — `.md-editor-wrap [contenteditable="true"] span { display: inline }` — that restores natural inline flow inside the editable area only (the toolbar's spans keep their `inline-block` layout). Do not remove that override, and do not loosen the `[contenteditable="true"]` qualifier — broadening it to all `.md-editor-wrap span` would break the toolbar. If the global rule is ever removed from `App.scss`, the override can come out too. Consumers pass `onChange` to capture edits and `onBlur` to persist — the canonical pattern is in [`UserPanelStoryTab.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/userPanel/UserPanelStoryTab/UserPanelStoryTab.js) and [`UserPanelMatchExplanation.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/userPanel/UserPanelMatchExplanation/UserPanelMatchExplanation.js). To reload the editor with a different initial value (e.g. switching to a different user's story), remount via a React `key` prop on `MarkdownEditor`; do not attempt to swap the value by changing `props.value`.
- User-facing field labels resolve via `getFieldName(isUserField, fieldData)` in `components/CustomField/helpers.js` → `users.fields.${label}` for user fields, raw `label` for custom fields.
- Zustand `persist` middleware (e.g. `useLocalStorageStore`) hydrates from `localStorage` **synchronously** at store creation. For UI state that drives transitioned/animated components (MUI Accordion, Collapse, Modal, Dialog), read the persisted value via a **lazy `useState` initializer** — `useState(() => useXxxStore.getState().getFoo(key))` — so the component's first render already has the saved value. Reading it inside a `useEffect` causes a `default → saved` state transition on mount, which triggers the component's open/close animation on first load.
- Suppressing MUI mount animations for persisted-state components (Accordion, etc.): lazy init alone is **not sufficient** — MUI's internal `Collapse` can still run a mount animation. Combine it with a `hasMountedRef = useRef(false)` + `useEffect(() => { hasMountedRef.current = true }, [])`, and pass `TransitionProps={{ timeout: hasMountedRef.current ? undefined : 0 }}`. First render → `timeout: 0` (no animation). After the user interacts, the re-render reads the now-`true` ref and restores the normal timeout. See `UserDetailsPanel.js` for the canonical pattern.
- AG-Grid column factories in [`src/Table/common/`](ob-love-admin-client/src/Table/common/) (`YesOrNo`, `String`, `DateTimeStamp`, `Date`, `Avatar`, `Img`, `Pin`) are the shared column builders — every page's `columns` array is composed of these. `formatValue` / `processData` controls rendering; it does **not** control sorting. When a column's real value lives off the data row (e.g., `params.data.meta_data.<field>` in `Fields.js`), the column must set a `valueGetter` so AG-Grid's sort comparator sees the extracted value — otherwise `data[field]` is `undefined` on every row and the sort is a no-op. `YesOrNo.js` handles this internally by forwarding `formatValue` into a `valueGetter`; any new factory that supports off-row data needs the same treatment.
- **Column resize policy — Google-Sheets-style freedom.** `Table.js`'s `defaultColDef` sets `resizable: true`, and data columns must not set `maxWidth` (it silently caps the drag and clamps `sizeColumnsToFit` distribution). `minWidth` floors come from a small bucket of constants in [`src/Table/common/columnWidths.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/Table/common/columnWidths.js), which reads its values from the `:export` block of [`src/styles/constants.scss`](ob-love-admin-client/src/styles/constants.scss): `NUMERIC` (60px — Age, Height), `ICON` (60px — Icon), `TEXT` (80px — String, Markdown, Email, Completion, plain text), `TAG` (90px — every tag-rendered cell, including YesOrNo), `DATE` (100px — Date, DateTimeStamp, ExpirePackageDate). Every shared factory under `src/Table/common/`, `src/Table/Users/`, `src/Table/CustomFields/`, `src/Table/Form/`, `src/Table/UserForm/`, etc. pulls from one of those buckets — never a literal pixel value. The only exceptions are utility/chrome columns (`Pin`, `MenuAction`, `DragDrop`, `Users/FindMatches`, `Users/Select`) which stay locked at 50px or 60px with `suppressSizeToFit: true`, and a handful of page-content-specific factories (`Form/LinkKey`, `Communication/User`, `Users/CustomField`) where the floor is content-derived. To keep cells from looking broken when the user drags below the floor (or a page sets a smaller floor), [`Tag.scss`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/Tag/Tag.scss) applies `min-width: 0; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap`, and [`EditableYesNo.scss`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/EditableYesNo/EditableYesNo.scss) uses `flex-wrap: nowrap` + `flex-shrink: 0` on the icon so the row never wraps.
- AG-Grid keyboard-focus management lives in [`src/Table/GridHelper.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/Table/GridHelper.js) — a static class that holds the grid/columnApi refs and is called from `Table.js` on grid-ready, body-scroll, and model-updated. Two invariants to preserve: (1) **focus must target a viewport-visible column, not `columns[0]`** — use `GridHelper.getFirstVisibleColumn()` (scans `getAllDisplayedColumns()` for the first column whose `getLeft() + getActualWidth() > getHorizontalPixelRange().left`); focusing `columns[0]` triggers `ensureColumnVisible` to snap horizontal scroll back to 0 when the user has scrolled right, and is the reason sort previously reset horizontal scroll while leaving vertical intact (`getFirstDisplayedRow()` is viewport-aware, `columns[0]` is not). (2) **`focus()` early-returns if a cell is already focused** — it's called from `onModelUpdated` (fires on every sort/filter), so re-setting focus on each update would cause a visible jump.
- **Drawer unsaved-changes detection** ([`store/drawerUnsaved/drawerUnsavedStore.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/store/drawerUnsaved/drawerUnsavedStore.js) + [`EditItemContainer.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/EditItemContainer/EditItemContainer.js) + [`SideDrawer.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/SideDrawer/SideDrawer.js)). `EditItemContainer` hashes `childrenData` (via `buildDataString`), captures the first hash as the baseline, and publishes `isUnsaved` to a global Zustand store; `SideDrawer.requestClose` reads it and shows the discard dialog when set. The baseline is taken **during the render** where `props.isInitialized` first flips to `true` — using a `useRef` set-once guard, not a `useEffect`. Reasoning: child effects fire before parent effects, so an effect-based capture races with the parent's hydrate effect; capturing during render reads the same `childrenData` the JSX is rendering with, so baseline and `currentDataString` agree by construction. Three layers must cooperate or the dialog falsely fires on a clean close:
  1. **The store owns `isInitialized`.** `formEditStore` flips it to `true` at the end of `initializeFields` (the last hydration step); `workflowEditStore` flips it at the end of `initializeWorkflow`; both reset to `false` in their `reset()`. Components read `<store>.isInitialized` and forward it via `isInitialized={...}`. Default for `EditItemContainer.isInitialized` is `true` so consumers that hold form state in plain component state ([`CustomFieldEdit`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/CustomField/CustomFieldEdit/CustomFieldEdit.js), [`EventEdit`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/event/EventEdit/EventEdit.js), [`PackageEditWrap`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/package/PackageEditWrap/PackageEditWrap.js), [`CreateTask`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/Task/CreateTask/CreateTask.js), [`UserProfile`](ob-love-admin-client/src/pages/UserProfile/UserProfile.js)) need no changes.
  2. **The drawer page must defer mount until data is loaded.** [`Form.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/pages/Form/Form.js) gates `<FormEdit>` behind `formStore.isFormLoading`; [`Workflows.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/pages/Workflows/Workflows.js) gates `<Workflow>` behind `workflowEditStore.isLoading`. Both render a `<Loading />` in the drawer body while the network fetch is in flight. **Without this guard, the edit component mounts with all props undefined, calls its store's `initialize*` action against empty data, flips `isInitialized=true` against the empty state, and `EditItemContainer` baselines the empty hash — then when the fetch resolves and props arrive populated, the next render shows a hash mismatch and the discard dialog fires on close.** The create flow is fine without the guard because `isLoading` is never set to `true` during create — there is nothing to load.
  3. **Don't mutate `childrenData` fields after init.** Anything in the `childrenData` object (FormEdit's `name`, `description`, `pages`, `target`, etc.; Workflow's `name`, `nodes`, `edges`, `isWorkflowActive`, `isNotify`) must not be mutated by mount-time effects after `initialize*` runs. `formEditStore.update('active', true)` from FormEdit's warning-message effect is safe only because `active` is *not* in `childrenData`; if you add a new auto-fixup that touches a `childrenData` field, the hash will drift and trigger a false positive.
  Any new drawer-hosted edit form that mirrors the hydrate-from-store pattern must implement all three: store-owned `isInitialized`, page-level `isLoading` mount gate, and no post-init mutations of fields included in the unsaved-changes hash.
- **Workflow editor — load/save round-trip invariants.** [`workflowLayout.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/workflow/workflowLayout.js) is the only place that converts between the backend `workflow_data` shape and React Flow canvas state. Four invariants the next person touching `workflowToReactFlow` / `convertFrontendToBackend` must preserve:
  1. **`workflow_data.steps` order is NOT meaningful.** `convertFrontendToBackend` emits steps in whatever order the React Flow node array happens to be in (insertion order, which changes as the user drops actions onto plus icons, splices via mid-edge plus, or drops onto a merge placeholder). Anything that needs "the action that follows the trigger" must derive it from the edge graph, not from `steps[0]`. The chain head is the one action node whose id never appears as a `to` in any backend edge. Picking `steps[0]` reloads a `Send Email → Delay → IF` workflow as a disjoint graph the moment the IF is persisted as the first step.
  2. **The trigger → first-action edge is NOT persisted.** The trigger lives outside `workflow_data.steps` (it's stored as `when_type` + `when_meta_data`) and `convertFrontendToBackend` strips every edge whose source is the trigger node. On load, `workflowToReactFlow` synthesizes that edge from the trigger to the chain head derived per (1). The trigger node's id is also fully derived from `when_type` (`whensMap[whenType].key`), so it's stable across reloads regardless of any frontend-only id state.
  3. **`workflow_data.edges` may carry phantom endpoints** referencing step ids that no longer exist (data drift, partial migrations, manual DB edits). `workflowToReactFlow` filters them out before they reach `layoutCanvas`; do not remove that filter — phantom edges propagate into `edgesBySource` and silently corrupt depth, lane, and tail computation downstream.
  4. **Unknown step `type` values must not be fatal.** `step.name = stepData.name` would throw on `undefined` if a `workflow_then_type` enum value is renamed or removed between deploys, taking the entire editor down for that workflow. The current loader drops unknown-typed steps with a `console.warn` and continues; do not introduce a new code path that touches `whensMap[step.type]` or `thensMap[step.type]` without the same guard.
  IF condition specifics: branches carry stable UUIDs (`b_<uuid>`) and edge `branch_key` references one of them. `getNodeData` only synthesizes default branches (fresh UUIDs) when `params.branches` is missing or empty — never when the array is non-empty — so non-empty backend payloads round-trip without losing branch identity. Removing a branch via the side panel cascades through `updateNodeMetadata` ([`workflowEditStore.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/store/workflow/workflowEditStore.js)), which does a reachability sweep from the trigger excluding edges with the removed `branch_key` and drops orphan nodes plus phantom edges. Deleting an action with `incoming === 1 && outgoing === 1` linear-restitches; deleting an IF, a merge target, or any node with multiple outgoing cascades through `deleteNode`. **Deleting the trigger (head, `outgoingCount > 0`, not linear) cascade-deletes the entire downstream chain → empty canvas; the user re-starts by dragging a new trigger. There is no "headless workflow" state and no leading plus icon — do not reintroduce one.**
- **Workflow React Flow handles are invisible structural anchors.** Every `<Handle>` in `NodeCanvas` / `NodePlusIcon` is `isConnectable={false}` — they exist only so edges have an attach point; the workflow has no user-drawn connections. `@xyflow/react/dist/style.css` (imported in `Workflow.js`) styles `.react-flow__handle` as a dark dot, so an unhidden handle shows as a stray black dot on a node. Two cooperating hides: `NodePlusIcon`'s source handle uses inline `style={{ visibility: 'hidden' }}`; all other handles are covered by the scoped `.react-flow-canvas .react-flow__handle { visibility: hidden }` rule in [`Workflow.scss`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/workflow/Workflow/Workflow.scss). Use `visibility: hidden`, not `display: none` — React Flow needs the element in layout to compute edge geometry. Don't add a visible-handle style unless user-drawn connections are actually introduced.
- **Hook order: state at the top, always.** Inside every function component: `useState` / `useReducer` / `useRef` come first (right after prop destructuring), then store reads, then `useMemo` / `useCallback`, then `useEffect`, then derived render variables, then `return`. State is the thing a reader needs to find first — burying it under `useMemo` blocks forces them to re-read the whole body to know what the component tracks. See [AGENTS.md](ob-love-admin-client/AGENTS.md) "Hook order inside a component" for the full rule.
- **`DialogWithActions` injects `setDialogParams` only — not `dialogParams`.** [`DialogWithActions.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/dialog/DialogWithActions/DialogWithActions.js) owns the `dialogParams` `useState`, clones the `content` element with `cloneElement(content, { setDialogParams })`, and passes only the resulting object to `actionCallback` / `validateDialogParams`. The child has **no read path back** — it only knows what it pushed. So any controlled input inside dialog content must hold its own local state and mirror it via `setDialogParams`; passing only an `onChange` that calls `setDialogParams` without a matching `value` on the input leaves the input in MUI's controlled mode with a stale `null`, which silently clears the user's selection on the next render (focus shift, popover close, parent re-render). [`FieldConditionEditor.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/FieldConditionEditor/FieldConditionEditor.js) is the canonical pattern — local `useState` for each value, mount-effect seeds `setDialogParams`, every `onXxxChange` updates both. Adding `dialogParams` to the clone payload would also work but would change every existing consumer's prop surface, so keep the local-state-mirror convention.
- **Dayjs values do not survive Zustand `persist`.** `JSON.stringify(dayjs())` uses dayjs's `toJSON` and emits an ISO string; on rehydration, `state.<dateField>` is a plain string (or a unix number if the code stored `.unix()`), **not** a dayjs instance. Passing a string to MUI `<DatePicker value={...}>` throws `e.isBefore is not a function` inside `AdapterDayjs.isBeforeDay` on first render — the adapter's internal min/max validation runs regardless of whether `minDate`/`maxDate` were supplied. Rule: persist dates as serializable primitives (unix number or ISO string), and re-wrap with `dayjs(value)` / `dayjs.unix(value)` **at the render site** every time. `CreateUserContent.js`'s birthday and custom-field date render paths are the canonical patterns; any new persisted date field must follow one of them.

**Filter pages — layout, search-query chips, focus pattern.** Every page that has a filterable list/board (`Users`, `Tasks`, `Packages`, `Funnel`, …) follows the same composition. Wire it like this:

- **Layout slot, not inline render.** The chip bar (filter icon + count + active-filter chips + reset ×) lives in the page's top bar, not in the body. Build it with `useMemo` as `<FilterQueryActions onClose={toggleShowFilter} onReset={resetFilter} isFiltering={filterVisible} showReset={hasFilters} searchQuery={<XxxSearchQuery .../>} />` and pass it to the layout via `setLayoutProps({ filtersSearchQuery, onToggleShowFilter, isEnableFilterShortcut })`. The body renders only the content (table / kanban / list). [Tasks.js](ob-love-admin-client/src/pages/Tasks/Tasks.js), [Packages.js](ob-love-admin-client/src/pages/Packages/Packages.js), and [Funnel.js](ob-love-admin-client/src/pages/Funnel/Funnel.js) are the canonical references.
- **Drawer.** The actual filter form lives in `<SearchFilter visible={filterVisible} handleClose={toggleShowFilter} module={MODULE.X} clearFocusFilterKey={...} focusFilterKey={...}>` rendered inside the page (sibling to the content). Pass it the page's `<XxxSearchFilters .../>` (a `SearchFilterLayout` with tabs).
- **Store contract.** The page's Zustand store must expose `filtersSelected`, `filterVisible`, `filterTab`, `focusFilterKey`, plus actions `toggleShowFilter(filterTab, focusFilterKey)`, `clearFocusFilterKey()`, `onFiltersSelected(newFilters)`, `resetFilter()`. The chips bar reads `filtersSelected` to render chips and `Object.keys(filtersSelected).length` to decide whether to show the reset ×.
- **Focus keys** ([components/QueryFilter/focusKeys.js](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/QueryFilter/focusKeys.js)). Each module has its own constant set: `USER_FILTER_FOCUS_KEYS`, `TASK_FILTER_FOCUS_KEYS`, `PACKAGE_FILTER_FOCUS_KEYS`, `FUNNEL_FILTER_FOCUS_KEYS`, etc. The keys are *strings*, shared between two places that must agree:
  1. **Chip generators** (`XxxSearchQuery.js`) attach a focus key to each chip via the 5th arg of `SearchQuery.generateKeyPhrase(label, value, onClick, customKey, focusKey)` / `generateKeyPhraseRange(...)`. The `onClick` itself is `(e, focusKey) => toggleShowFilters(tab, focusKey || defaultFocusKey)` — `QueryPhrase` calls it with the chip's own focus key as the 2nd arg.
  2. **Filter tab inputs** (`XxxLeadDetailsTab.js`, `SearchTab.js`, …) declare a `useRef` per focusable input, build a `refMap = { [FOCUS_KEY]: ref, ... }`, and run `useEffect(() => { refMap[props.focusFilterKey]?.current?.focus?.() }, [])` exactly once on mount. The empty deps array is correct because the tab is **remounted on every drawer open** via `key={`xxx_filters_wrap_${filterVisible}`}` on the wrapping `<XxxSearchFilters>`. Without that key, the `useEffect` would not re-run when re-opening with a different focus key.
- **Focus key flow.** Chip click → `toggleShowFilter(tab, focusKey)` → store sets `{ filterVisible: true, filterTab, focusFilterKey }` → `<SearchFilter>` becomes visible with `focusFilterKey` prop → `<XxxSearchFilters key={...filterVisible}>` remounts, passes `focusFilterKey` down to the active tab → tab's mount-effect focuses the matching ref → `SearchFilter`'s 100ms `setTimeout(clearFocusFilterKey)` clears it so a repeat click on the same chip refocuses.
- **Naming.** Tab files in `components/<module>Filters/<module>FiltersTabs/<TabName>/`. Tab key constants in `components/<module>Filters/helpers.js` (e.g. `TAB_FUNNEL_LEAD_DETAILS_FILTERS`).

**Floating dialog shell — `FloatingDialog` is the single owner of drag/resize/persist.** [`FloatingDialog`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/FloatingDialog/FloatingDialog.js) is the shared draggable + resizable, `createPortal`-to-`document.body` dialog. It owns: position/size persistence to localStorage via `useLocalStorageStore.getSearchFilterState/setSearchFilterState` keyed by a `storageKey` prop (drag/resize are no-ops when `storageKey` is null), the ESC/ENTER global keydown close (`closeOnEsc` default true, `closeOnEnter` default false), the `BackDrop`, and the header (`headerIcon` + `title` + `headerActions` + `CloseIconButton`). [`SearchFilter`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/QueryFilter/SearchFilter/SearchFilter.js) is now a **thin wrapper** around it (passes `storageKey={props.module}`, `TuneIcon`, the filter title, `closeOnEnter={true}`, and keeps the `GridHelper.focus()`-on-close + `clearFocusFilterKey` timeout that are filter-specific). The saved-queries list ([`SavedQueriesDialog`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/userFilters/SavedQueries/SavedQueriesDialog.js)) is the second wrapper. Do **not** re-implement drag/resize/localStorage in a new floating dialog — wrap `FloatingDialog`. The CSS classes are `.floating-dialog*` in [`FloatingDialog.scss`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/FloatingDialog/FloatingDialog.scss) (the old `.search-filters*` selectors were retired; nothing else referenced them — `SearchFilterLayout`'s `.search-filters-tabs` is an independent class, not an ancestor dependency).

**Saved filter queries** (Users module). [`SavedQueriesActions`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/userFilters/SavedQueries/SavedQueriesActions.js) sits in `Users.js`'s `filterQueryAdditionalActions` (the chip-bar) and renders two icons: a bookmark save toggle (`BookmarkBorderOutlined` → not saved, `BookmarkAdded` → current filters match a saved query, decided by `isCurrentQuerySaved` via `stableStringify`) and a `BookmarksOutlined` "view list" button opening `SavedQueriesDialog`. Save/edit use `showSaveQueryDialog`/`showEditSavedQueryDialog` in [`store/window/helpers.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/store/window/helpers.js) (DialogWithActions + `SavedQueryForm`, the canonical local-state-mirror-`setDialogParams` pattern). The list-row preview reuses `<UserSearchQuery readOnly hideInitialCount>` (same read-only path `DefaultFiltersButton` uses — `QueryPhrase` hard-blocks clicks when `readOnly`). Clicking a row applies `query` via `usersState.onFiltersSelected(cloneFilters(query))` + `resetGrid()`. State in [`savedQueriesStore`](ob-love-admin-client/src/store/savedQueries/savedQueriesStore.js); REST via [`networkSavedQueries`](ob-love-admin-client/src/network/networkSavedQueries.js) (`Network.savedQueries.*`, registered through the side-effect import in the store). All feature logic (saved-state check, creator/shared-status labels, payload builders, validation) lives in [`SavedQueries/helpers.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/userFilters/SavedQueries/helpers.js); the 3-dots row menu builder is in [`MenuItem/SavedQueries/menuItems.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/MenuItem/SavedQueries/menuItems.js). The shared-status enum is **not** redeclared on the client — it reads `useSystemStore.getState().constants.shared_status` (backend `AdminSavedQueries.SharedStatus`, published via `update_constants`; `systemStore` initial state carries the `shared_status: {}` placeholder); enum labels resolve through `enums.sharedStatus.<lowercased_key>`.

**User fields categorization** (admin UI for listing / creating / searching users — separate system from the form-builder below):

- The user-side organization of built-in + custom fields lives in `uiSettings[MODULE.USER].profileCard.fieldsOrder`. Shape: `{ [categoryKey]: { label, subCategories: [{ key?, label, fields: [...] }] } }`. Fields are stored as raw keys — user-field names (e.g. `"gender"`) or `custom_field_<id>` strings — **not** objects.
- The **`general` category** is special and has three reserved subcategories keyed `profile`, `search`, `customFields` (see `GENERAL_KEY` / `GENERAL_SUBCATEGORIES` in [`components/User/fieldOrderHelpers.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/User/fieldOrderHelpers.js)). `PROFILE_FIELDS` / `SEARCH_FIELDS` constants in the same file enumerate which built-in user fields auto-land in `general > profile` vs. `general > search`. `general > customFields` is the default bucket for any custom field not yet dragged elsewhere.
- [`buildCustomFieldCategoryMap(fieldsOrder, customFieldsData)`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/CustomField/helpers.js) walks `fieldsOrder`, hydrates each `custom_field_<id>` string into the full field object, and returns `{ mappedSubCategories, unmappedFields }`. A subcategory lands in `mappedSubCategories` iff it contains at least one custom **or** built-in field — **except** `general > customFields`, which is always skipped so its contents flow into `unmappedFields`. Each entry exposes `categoryKey`, `subKey`, `categoryLabel`, `subLabel`, a display `label` (deduped with `" (CategoryLabel)"` suffix when two subcategories share the same label), `fields` (hydrated custom-field objects), `builtinFieldNames` (raw built-in field keys like `"gender"` / `"email"` that the user placed into this subcategory), and `orderedFields` (the interleaved sequence — entries are `{ kind: 'custom', field }` or `{ kind: 'builtin', name }` in the exact order the user arranged them in `fieldsOrder`; consume this when render order matters, e.g. the manual-create tabs). Consumers that only want custom fields filter on `category.fields.length > 0` themselves (e.g. [`UserSearchFilters`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/userFilters/UserSearchFilters/UserSearchFilters.js) skips built-in-only subcategories when building its filter tabs).
- **Keys vs. translated labels — easy to get wrong.** `categoryKey` / `subKey` are the internal string IDs (`"general"`, `"profile"`, `"search"`, `"customFields"`, …). `categoryLabel` / `subLabel` / `label` are the user-facing i18n strings (e.g. `"algemeen"` in Dutch, `"פרופיל"` in Hebrew). Always compare against `GENERAL_KEY` / `GENERAL_SUBCATEGORIES.*` using the `*Key` fields — the label-based comparison accidentally works in English (where `i18n.t('general.general') === 'general'`) and silently breaks in every other locale.
- [`CreateUserContent.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/User/CreateUserContent/CreateUserContent.js) composes its tabs as: (1) a hardcoded "Profile" tab that renders every built-in user field plus any custom fields whose category/subcategory keys are `general` + (`profile`|`search`), (2) an "Unmapped custom fields" tab fed by `unmappedCustomFields`, (3) one tab per remaining entry in `mappedCustomFieldSubCategories`. `general > profile` and `general > search` are explicitly *not* turned into separate tabs — their fields are inlined into the built-in Profile tab instead. Uses the `*Key` fields for that filter. **Built-in fields follow fieldsOrder too**: when a user drags a built-in field (e.g. `height`, `is_smoking`) into a non-general-profile/search subcategory, the component's `relocatedBuiltinFields` set suppresses it in the Profile layout and its tab's render path pulls it in via `builtinFieldElementByName[name]`. Only fields present in that registry can be relocated; fields that the creation form doesn't render at all (e.g. `status`, `created_at`) are silently ignored. **Tab body order follows `category.orderedFields`** (not `fields` then `builtinFieldNames`), so a subcategory laid out as `[customA, gender, customB]` in `fieldsOrder` renders in that exact sequence inside the tab. The unmapped tab synthesizes a `[{ kind: 'custom', field }]` array for the same render path so all non-Profile tabs go through one renderer.
- [`Users.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/pages/Users/Users.js) builds two variants off the same hook: the full `{mappedSubCategories, unmappedFields}` for the list/filter grid, plus a filtered `*ForManualCreate` pair that drops any field whose `meta_data.is_show_on_manual_user_create` is falsy — that filtered pair is what `CreateUserContent` receives. The filter walks `fields`, `builtinFieldNames`, **and** `orderedFields` in lockstep so all three stay aligned post-filter.

**Per-field searchable overrides** — admin marks a custom field as a default filter that gets merged into Find Matches results.

- **Storage.** [`uiSettings[MODULE.USER].profileCard.searchableFields`](ob-love-admin-client/src/store/uiSettings/uiSettingsStore.js) — keyed by the field's `field` string (`custom_field_<id>`); each entry is `{ comparison, value, type }` where `type` is the `field_type` constant ID. Listed under `LEAF_KEYS` so it persists with the rest of `uiSettings`. **Only custom fields go in this map** — `removeSearchableField` and `pruneSearchableForCustomFields` both early-return on built-in user-field keys via `isFieldKeyCustomField`.
- **Eligibility.** TEXT, TEXT_AREA, DROPDOWN, MULTI_SELECT custom fields only — gated by `_isFieldSearchableEligible` in [`store/uiSettings/helpers.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/store/uiSettings/helpers.js). Other types (NUMBER, DATE, YES_NO, PHOTO, RANGE, ADDRESS, PHONE_NUMBER, VIDEO) get neither the icon nor the menu items.
- **`SEARCH_FIELDS` are NOT searchable fields — easy to confuse.** `SEARCH_FIELDS` in [`fieldOrderHelpers.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/User/fieldOrderHelpers.js) (`age_from`/`age_to`, `age_years_younger`/`age_years_older`, `height_from_cm`/`height_to_cm`, `want_smoking`, `location`, `location_radius`) is the list of user **preference** fields — what the user is *looking for in matches*, hard-wired into `buildFindMatchesFilter` on every find-matches call. They never live in `searchableFields`, never get the icon, and never appear in the searchable menu. The constant is module-private to `fieldOrderHelpers.js` — don't export it.
- **Find-matches flow.** [`Users.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/pages/Users/Users.js)'s `findMatches(userId, userData)`: (1) build base filter via `buildFindMatchesFilter(userData, {is_lgbtq, is_use_age_range, default_orientation, systemConstants})` — pulls the user's preference fields (`SEARCH_FIELDS`) and gender/orientation logic. (2) Read `searchableFields` from `useUiSettingsStore`. (3) `applySearchableOverrides(baseFilter, searchableFields)` merges in the per-field overrides as `merged['custom_field_<id>'] = { value, type }`. (4) Pass to `usersStore.onFiltersSelected(filterData, false, true, userId)` with `isFindMatches=true`.
- **DROPDOWN / MULTI_SELECT array contract — must-know.** `filtersSelected['custom_field_<id>'].value` for DROPDOWN/MULTI_SELECT must be an **array** — the chip renderer at [`UserSearchQuery.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/userFilters/UserSearchQuery/UserSearchQuery.js) line ~514 does `value.map(...).join(', ')`, and the existing filter UI at [`CustomFieldsTab.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/userFilters/userSearchFiltersTabs/CustomFieldsTab/CustomFieldsTab.js) writes them with `multiple={true}`. The `MarkSearchableDialog` UI stores DROPDOWN as a single string (`LabeledDropdown multiple={false}`) and MULTI_SELECT as an array, so `applySearchableOverrides` normalizes by wrapping non-array values to `[value]` for both types before merging. Without that wrap → `TypeError: value.map is not a function`.
- **UI surface.** [`UserDetailsPanel.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/User/UserDetailsPanel/UserDetailsPanel.js) renders the magnifying-glass `SearchableIcon` ([`assets/icons/SearchableIcon.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/assets/icons/SearchableIcon.js)) inside `.user-details-menu-wrap`, to the **left** of the three-dot menu (not inside the field-name area), when `isFieldSearchable(field, searchableFields)` is true. The row's menu is composed as `[...categorySubCategoryMenuItems, ...buildSearchableFieldMenuItems(field, searchableFields)]`, so existing menu items stay first and "mark / edit / remove searchable" append.
- **Comparison options — currently equals only.** `comparisonOptions` in [`MarkSearchableDialog.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/User/MarkSearchableDialog/MarkSearchableDialog.js) returns `[_opt('equals')]` for all four eligible types. The `COMPARISON_LABELS` map and the `general.contains` / `general.startsWith` / `general.endsWith` / `general.in` i18n keys are intentionally kept — re-introducing other comparisons is just adding entries to that array. **Backend currently only honors equality on custom fields**, so even if the UI sent another comparison it would be ignored at match time.
- **Pruning stale entries.** [`fieldsStore.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/store/fields/fieldsStore.js) calls `useUiSettingsStore.getState().pruneSearchableForCustomFields(customFieldsData)` after init / delete / reorder. The store action delegates to [`pruneSearchableForCustomFields`](ob-love-admin-client/src/store/uiSettings/helpers.js) which drops any entry whose `custom_field_<id>` no longer exists or whose stored `type` no longer matches the current field's type. The helper returns `{ cleaned, changed }` — the action short-circuits the store update when `changed === false` to avoid spurious rerenders.
- **Dialog wiring.** `showMarkSearchableDialog(field, existing, onSubmit)` in [`store/window/helpers.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/store/window/helpers.js) wraps the dialog in `DialogWithActions`. Save is gated by `validate(params) → !isEmptyValue(params.value)`. The validate fallback to `initial?.value` when `params.value === undefined` exists so the Save button doesn't flicker disabled→enabled when *re*opening a previously-marked field — `MarkSearchableDialog`'s mount effect seeds `dialogParams` asynchronously, so the first validate pass before the effect runs would otherwise fail. The dialog's preview-pill chips use `.search-query-wrapper` / `.query-phrase` from the global `SearchQuery.scss`, with a scoped override in [`MarkSearchableDialog.scss`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/User/MarkSearchableDialog/MarkSearchableDialog.scss) to allow the chips to wrap below a long field label (the global `.query-phrase` is fixed-height inline-flex).

**Form-builder helpers** (all in [`components/Form/helpers.js`](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/Form/helpers.js)) — every form-builder component goes through these, do not reimplement:

| Helper | Use when |
|---|---|
| `buildNewFormPageField(rawField, systemConstants)` | A raw `CustomField`/`UserFieldDefinition` from the palette is being turned into a `form_page_field` row (drag-drop, menu-add, mandatory-field seeding). Returns the full shape: `target_field_id`, `target_field_type`, `name`, `meta_data`, `is_user_field`, and the aliased `custom_field_*` / `user_field_*` columns so pre-save rendering matches post-reload. **Single source of truth — all four "add field" code paths call it.** |
| `isFormPageFieldUserField(field, systemConstants)` | Deciding user vs custom for a `form_page_field` row. Handles both saved rows (uses `target_field_type`) and unsaved rows (uses `is_user_field`). |
| `getFormPageFieldLabel(field, isUserField)` | Producing the i18n key for a `form_page_field`'s display label. Walks `user_field_label` → `custom_field_label` → `label` → `field.name`. |
| `canFieldBeHidden(field, allowed_types)` | Checking whether a field can be dropped onto a hidden page. Reads `.type` (raw palette field) OR `.custom_field_type` / `.user_field_type` (form_page_field row). |
| `isMandatoryFieldsPresent(pageData, mandatoryFields)` | Warning-message gate in `formEditStore.updateWarningMessage`. Checks `field.user_field_label || field.label` against `mandatoryUserFields`. |
| `buildUserFields(fieldsData, is_lgbtq)`, `buildMandatoryUserFields(fieldsData, is_lgbtq)` | Populating the palette and mandatory-field list at init. Filters by `field.label` (not `field.meta_data.key`). |

**FormPage / FormPageFieldDisplay / renderFormField** — layered responsibility:
- `FormPage.js` (per-page renderer) computes `isUserField` once via the helper and passes it down as a prop.
- `FormPageFieldDisplay.js` is **dumb** — takes `isUserField` from props, calls `getFormPageFieldLabel` for its label, and uses that label for both the visible label and the disabled placeholder input.
- `renderFormField.js` (the end-user form renderer) computes `isUserField` + `fieldLabel` once at the top and uses `fieldLabel` for every `label` / `placeholder` across all field-type branches (text, date, dropdown, photo, range sub-fields, orientation).

**Rename note (important for next session).** The `form_page_field.field_type` column was renamed to **`target_field_type`** across the stack, and the enum class `FormFieldType` was renamed to **`FormTargetFieldType`**. Consequences:
- Frontend reads `field.target_field_type` (saved) or `field.is_user_field` (unsaved).
- systemConstants key is `form_target_field_type` (derived from the snake-cased class name).
- `systemConstants.field_type` on the client is a **different** enum — `CustomField.FieldType` (TEXT / NUMBER / DATE / DROPDOWN / YES_NO / PHOTO / …). Don't conflate the two.

**Drag & drop flow** (form editor): four entry points end up calling `buildNewFormPageField`:
- `FormEdit.onDragEnd` → regular page: inline `newField = buildNewFormPageField(...)`, then `formEditStore.updateFieldsOrder(..., newField)`.
- `FormEdit.onDragEnd` → hidden page: `formEditStore.updateFieldsWithHiddenPage(...)` builds it internally.
- Menu "Add to page": `formEditStore.addFieldToPage(...)` builds it.
- Menu "Add to hidden page": `formEditStore.addFieldToHiddenPage(...)` builds it.

## `ob-love-ui` — shared React component library

**What it does.** The `@ob-love/ui` package — reusable UI primitives, helpers, utilities, and SCSS tokens consumed by `ob-love-admin-client` (and future ob-love apps). Anything that is genuinely cross-app and domain-free moves here; we'll keep adding to it as we go.

**Stack.** React 18, MUI 5, dayjs, react-markdown, `react-phone-input-2`, `@uiw/react-md-editor`. Built with `tsup`; published locally via `yalc` (`npm run publish:local` → `npm run dev` watches and pushes).

**Top-level layout** (relative to `ob-love-ui/`):
- `src/components/<Name>/<Name>.{js,scss}` — one folder per component (see `src/index.js` for the export list: `Tag`, `Badge`, `Heading`, `Loading`, `AvatarInfoSmall`, `IconLabel`, `AutocompleteDropdown`, `DropDownMenu`, `EditMode*` editable inputs, `Labeled*` inputs, `LabeledAddressField`, `DateRangePicker`, …).
- `src/assets/svg/` — illustration SVGs the lib ships.
- `src/utils.js`, `src/i18n.js`, `src/QueueCallbackRunner.js`, `src/theme.js` — common helpers, the i18n setter (`setI18n`), an async queue runner, and the MUI theme factory (`createObLoveTheme`, `inputOptionsNumberSpinner`, `tabsUnderLine`).
- **i18n bridge contract.** `src/i18n.js` is *not* a re-export of i18next — it's a small proxy whose default export only exposes the methods explicitly forwarded to the client-injected instance (today: `t`, `dir`). The client wires its singleton in via `setI18n(i18n)` at startup (`ob-love-admin-client/src/i18n.js`), and library code reads through the proxy. If a library file needs another i18next method (`language`, `changeLanguage`, `exists`, …), **add it to the bridge first** — calling it on the default export without proxying it throws `i18n.<x> is not a function` at runtime. Never `import i18next from 'i18next'` directly from inside `ob-love-ui` — the symlink/yalc dedupe trap is the whole reason the bridge exists.
- `styles/constants.scss` — design tokens (`$COLOR-*`, `$SIZE-*`, `$FONT-*`, plus the JS `:export` block consumed via `import constants from '@ob-love/ui/styles/constants.scss'`). All hex/px constants for the whole stack live here — see the SCSS Styling rules in [ob-love-admin-client/AGENTS.md](ob-love-admin-client/AGENTS.md) for the do-not-hardcode policy.
- `styles/common.scss`, `styles/img-field.scss`, `styles/scrollbar.scss` — shared global styles.

**Consumer pattern in `ob-love-admin-client`.**
- Imports are `import { Loading, AutocompleteDropdown, LabeledTextField, ... } from '@ob-love/ui'`. Constants come in via `import constants from '@ob-love/ui/styles/constants.scss'`.
- Don't duplicate a primitive locally if a `@ob-love/ui` export already covers it — extend the upstream component or wrap it instead. Domain-named props don't belong on these primitives (see "Generic UI components must not know domain concepts" in the AGENTS file).
- When promoting a primitive from `ob-love-admin-client` to `ob-love-ui`: move the component folder, add an export to `ob-love-ui/src/index.js`, run `npm run publish:local`, and update the client imports. Domain helpers (anything that references admin/users/forms/etc.) stay in the client.

**Component API gotchas.**
- **`LabeledToggleButton` takes `initialValue`, not `value`.** Despite the name, it behaves like a controlled input — internal state is re-synced whenever the `initialValue` prop changes (`useEffect` in [LabeledToggleButton.js](ob-love-ui/src/components/LabeledToggleButton/LabeledToggleButton.js)). Passing `value={...}` silently does nothing: the prop is ignored, `internalValue` stays `undefined`, `ToggleButtonGroup` receives no `value`, and the active button never highlights even though `onChange` keeps firing. Every caller in `ob-love-admin-client` uses `initialValue=` — match that.
- **`AutocompleteDropdown` keeps the popup open after selection in `multiple` mode.** [AutocompleteDropdown.js](ob-love-ui/src/components/AutocompleteDropdown/AutocompleteDropdown.js) hard-codes `disableCloseOnSelect={props.multiple}` on the underlying MUI `<Autocomplete>`. MUI's own default for that flag is `false` — meaning the popup closes on every option click, even in `multiple` mode, which fires `onClose` and (for paginated wrappers like [UserSelectAutocompleteDropdown](ob-love-admin-client/src/components/UserSelectAutocompleteDropdown/UserSelectAutocompleteDropdown.js)) tears down the in-flight `searchResults` mid-selection. The wrapper's `onClose` handler runs in the same React batch as the parent's `setFormValues` from `onChange`; if it clears the option list before the chip renders, the just-selected id resolves to nothing and no chip mounts. Rules for any multi-select wrapper that holds local state tied to the open dropdown (search results, pagination, accumulated pages):
  1. **Rely on `disableCloseOnSelect` staying on for `multiple`** — don't override it back to `false` per call site.
  2. **Don't put aggressive teardown in the wrapper's `onClose`.** No `setSearchResults([])`, no `setInputValue('')`. Only clear cross-component side effects (e.g. store filters) there.
  3. **Don't write to `inputValue` on `onInputChange` `reason !== 'input'`.** With `disableCloseOnSelect` on, MUI fires `'reset'` not just after a selection but also on options/value re-syncs that happen mid-typing — clearing `inputValue` there wipes the user's text on every keystroke. Gate `setInputValue(value)` behind `reason === 'input'`; leave the rest alone.

**Peer-dep contract.** Everything MUI/dayjs/react-markdown/etc. is a `peerDependency` — `ob-love-ui` does not bundle React or MUI; the consuming app supplies them. Adding a new heavy dep to this lib is a deliberate decision (it grows the peer-dep surface of every consumer), so prefer pulling in functionality the consumer already has.

## `pay-core-lib` — payment gateway integration

**What it does.** Captures and persists payment transactions from the PayPlus gateway (legacy Yaadpay tables also tracked). Provides the URL generator for hosted checkout, the success/verification webhook handler, and the read queries the admin app needs for billing.

**Owned tables.** `payment_pay_plus_info`, `payment_info_hyp` (legacy Yaadpay; not actively written to).

**Key modules.**
- [`pay_core_lib/pay_core_lib.py`](pay-core-lib/pay_core_lib/pay_core_lib.py) — composition root. Exposes `self.payment` (a `PaymentPayPlusService`) and `self.auth` (the `ClientPayPlus` HTTP client). Parent apps reach payment via `<their_core_lib>.pay.payment.<method>(...)`.
- [`pay_core_lib/data_layers/service/payment_pay_plus_service.py`](pay-core-lib/pay_core_lib/data_layers/service/payment_pay_plus_service.py) — `get_url(...)` (hosted-checkout URL), `payment_success(...)` (webhook handler — verifies, persists, fires `EVENT_PAYMENT_SUCCESS`), `all_recurring_payments()`, `all_by_project(project_id)`, `get_latest_by_project(project_id)` (the billing-page reads), `update(id, data)`.
- [`pay_core_lib/data_layers/data_access/payment_pay_plus_data_access.py`](pay-core-lib/pay_core_lib/data_layers/data_access/payment_pay_plus_data_access.py) — soft-delete CRUD plus the `all_by_project` / `get_latest_by_project` / `all_recurring_payments` queries.
- [`pay_core_lib/data_layers/client/pay_plus/client_pay_plus.py`](pay-core-lib/pay_core_lib/data_layers/client/pay_plus/client_pay_plus.py) — outbound HTTP to PayPlus.
- `pay_core_lib/observer/pay_listener.py` + `pay_observer_listener.py` — observer pattern; consumers register a `PayListener` via `PayCoreLib.attach_pay_listener(...)` to react to `EVENT_PAYMENT_SUCCESS`.

**`pay_core_lib.constants.CurrencyCode` + `CurrencySymbol`.** Two paired enums in [`pay_core_lib/constants.py`](pay-core-lib/pay_core_lib/constants.py). `CurrencyCode` is the storage/transport enum (`ILS = 'ILS'`, `USD = 'USD'`, `EUR = 'EUR'`, `GBP = 'GBP'`) — `.value` is what gets written to the `currency` VARCHAR column and sent to the PayPlus gateway. `CurrencySymbol` is a plain class-based enum whose member names mirror `CurrencyCode` — a module-level `assert set(CurrencySymbol.__members__) == set(CurrencyCode.__members__)` catches drift at import time. Pair them via `CurrencySymbol[code.name].value` → `'$'` / `'₪'` / `'€'` / `'£'`. Both ship to the client via `AdminSystemService.get_admin()` → `update_constants(...)` so the client sees them as `systemStore.constants.currency_code` and `systemStore.constants.currency_symbol`. **Not the same map as `objective_love_core_lib.constants.package_constants.currency_symbols`** (plural, integer-keyed, used by package pricing) — pay-side flows always read from these two enums.

`CurrencyCode` used to be nested inside `PaymentPayPlusInfo` for historical reasons; it was lifted to `constants.py` so `CurrencySymbol` can reference it without a circular import (the entity already imports `DEFAULT_SYSTEM` from `constants.py`). All consumers — `payment_pay_plus_service`, `client_pay_plus`, `admin_payment_service`, `admin_billing_service` — now import `CurrencyCode` directly from `pay_core_lib.constants`.
